close_reason: Implemented LeaseExtender module that periodically extends leases during job execution. Manager now spawns an extender alongside each job task and stops it when the job completes. Default interval is lease_duration/3 (10s for 30s lease).
description:
**QuiCK Paper Reference:** Algorithm 3, lines 10-12

**Current Behavior:**
Workers execute jobs without extending their lease. If a job takes longer than the lease duration (default 30s), the lease expires and the item becomes visible again, causing duplicate execution.

**Expected Behavior (per QuiCK):**
```
do in parallel
  Periodically extend_lease(item) in QDB
```

A separate thread/task should periodically extend the lease while the job is being processed.

**Files to modify:**
- `apps/bedrock_job_queue/lib/bedrock/job_queue/consumer/manager.ex`
- `apps/bedrock_job_queue/lib/bedrock/job_queue/consumer/worker.ex`

**Risk:** High - Long jobs will be executed multiple times
title: Add parallel lease extension for long-running jobs
